title: Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) in Agri Economics
content: The Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) in Agricultural Economics is a dynamic and intellectually stimulating program that delves into the financial, policy, and management aspects of agriculture and rural development. Typically spanning 3 to 5 years, this program is designed for individuals with a Master’s degree in Agricultural Economics, Economics, or related fields, who have a keen interest in analyzing agricultural markets, resource allocation, and economic sustainability. Admission requires 55%–60% marks, along with qualifying exams such as ICAR AICE JRF/SRF, UGC-NET, or CSIR-NET, followed by an entrance test, interview, and research proposal submission. Scholars engage in cutting-edge research on topics like agribusiness management, rural finance, agricultural trade policies, environmental economics, and food security, using advanced econometric and statistical tools. With a blend of theoretical insights and practical applications, this program equips graduates to become policymakers, economic analysts, consultants, and academicians, contributing to institutions such as universities, research organizations, agribusiness firms, financial institutions, and global bodies like FAO, World Bank, and NABARD. Whether shaping agricultural policies or driving economic innovation in rural development, Ph.D. scholars in Agricultural Economics play a crucial role in transforming the future of agrarian economies.
